ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for lontong goreng; mapped to the closest parent class 'fried rice dish' (FOODON_03315595) as lontong goreng is a stir-fried compressed rice cake dish. Exact term does not exist in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00GJ (rice-based composite dish) used as the closest defensible anchor. No specific lontong goreng entry exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept identified for lontong goreng or fried compressed rice cake as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of lontong goreng is approximately 250 g (one plate), comprising stir-fried compressed rice cake pieces with minced meat, egg, chilli, and seasoning.

nutrient_source.title: Lontong Goreng — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Lontong Goreng (F05100074). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this entry at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous fried rice dish (SR Legacy #20045) and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for lontong goreng exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 72 (high) based on the closest analogous food: white rice (jasmine/short-grain, cooked), GI ~72 per University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). Lontong is compressed steamed white rice with a dense, gelatinised starch structure; the stir-frying process does not substantially lower GI relative to the white rice base. Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (72 × 29.6) / 100 ≈ 21.3.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (352 mg/100 g) from belacan and soy sauce, with low potassium (~130 mg/100 g) and insufficient dietary fibre (1.52 g/100 g) to offset sodium load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit lontong goreng to small portions and request preparation with reduced belacan and soy sauce; pairing with potassium-rich vegetables such as kangkong or spinach can partially offset the sodium burden.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI compressed white rice base (estimated GI ~72) with only 1.52 g/100 g dietary fibre, resulting in rapid postprandial glucose elevation and a glycemic load of ~21 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume lontong goreng in small portions, request a reduced lontong-to-vegetable ratio, and avoid kecap manis (sweet soy sauce) additions that further increase the glycemic and sugar load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (352 mg/100 g) and moderate fat (8.09 g/100 g including ~2.1 g saturated fat) from stir-frying with animal-based ingredients; low dietary fibre limits cardiovascular benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size, request reduced oil and soy sauce in preparation, and pair with fibre-rich vegetables to partially mitigate the sodium and saturated fat contribution.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Contains chilli (nightshade); solanine may aggravate joint inflammation in RA-sensitive individuals; elevated sodium (352 mg/100 g) may worsen tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to nightshades should request chilli-free preparation and reduced belacan and soy sauce to lower sodium and potential inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lontong goreng is a Singaporean and Malaysian Malay stir-fried dish made from lontong — compressed steamed white rice cake cut into bite-sized pieces — wok-fried with minced meat (typically chicken or beef), egg, chilli, belacan (fermented shrimp paste), and soy sauce. The dish has a savoury, slightly spicy flavour profile with a firm, chewy rice texture contrasting with the tender minced meat and aromatic seasoning. It is commonly served as a snack or light meal at Malay hawker stalls and food courts across Singapore and Malaysia. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 149 kcal, 7.25 g protein, 8.09 g fat, and 11.85 g carbohydrate, with 352 mg sodium and 1.52 g dietary fibre. The high-GI white rice base (estimated GI ~72) and elevated sodium from belacan and soy sauce make this dish a moderate-to-high concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. Calcium (65 mg/100 g) and iron (2.68 mg/100 g) are notable micronutrients. The dish is halal-certified in most hawker contexts but contains crustacean allergens (belacan) and soy.
common_user_queries: How many calories in lontong goreng?
Is lontong goreng suitable for diabetics?
Is lontong goreng high in sodium?
Can I eat lontong goreng if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F05100074) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous fried rice entry. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous white rice entry with documented basis. All mandatory multilingual names present for Singaporean Malay cuisine.
notes: Saturated fat (2.1 g/100 g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for fried rice with meat (closest approved-source analogue), as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this entry. Potassium (~130 mg/100 g) is an estimate from USDA FoodData Central analogous fried rice entry; HPB does not report potassium for this item. Fields set to -1 (monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g): detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for lontong goreng are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this specific preparation as of 2026. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on white rice (jasmine/short-grain) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for lontong goreng exists in any approved GI source. Dietary tag 'low_calorie' from input preserved in cultural context; at 149 kcal/100 g this is moderate-calorie for a rice-based dish.
